children.heading: .—Bonus Authorized; Bonus Amount
children.label: a .—Bonus Authorized; Bonus Amount
children.text: A person who enlists in an armed force for a period of at least 2 years may be paid a bonus in an amount not to exceed $40,000. The bonus may be paid in a single lump sum or in periodic installments.

children.heading: .—Repayment
children.label: b .—Repayment
children.text: A member who does not complete the term of enlistment for which a bonus was paid to the member under this section, or a member who is not technically qualified in the skill for which a bonus was paid to the member under this section, shall be subject to the repayment provisions of .section 303a(e) of this title

children.heading: .—Relation to Prohibition on Bounties
children.label: c .—Relation to Prohibition on Bounties
children.text: The enlistment bonus authorized by this section is not a bounty for purposes of .section 514(a) of title 10

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: d .—Regulations
children.text: This section shall be administered under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense for the armed forces under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense and by the Secretary of Homeland Security for the Coast Guard when the Coast Guard is not operating as a service in the Navy.

children.heading: .—Duration of Authority
children.label: e .—Duration of Authority
children.text: No bonus shall be paid under this section with respect to any enlistment in the armed forces made after .December 31, 2018
